sanskrit:
श्रेयान् द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप।
सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते॥
transliteration: śreyān dravyamayād yajñāj jñāna-yajñaḥ parantapa | sarvaṃ karmākhilaṃ pārtha jñāne parisamāpyate ||
literal_meaning: Better than the sacrifice of wealth, O chastiser of foes, is the sacrifice of knowledge; every action without exception, O son of Pṛthā, culminates in knowledge.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s anxiety about ‘doing the right thing’ by revealing that the ultimate goal of any action is not the act itself but the inner clarity it can generate. All rituals, jobs, or moral choices are only stepping-stones; when they mature into self-knowledge they become ‘spiritual’.
psychological_state: Cognitive defusion from outcome-based self-worth; shift from performance anxiety to exploratory learning mindset.
trigger_scenario: A perfectionist employee realizes that ticking every KPI box feels empty; they begin journaling lessons learned each day, turning work into a lab for self-understanding rather than a stage for self-validation.
anchor_text: Let every deed refine into knowing who you truly are.
